methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terra Drone operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector as a publicly listed company on the Tokyo exchange. The comparable universe for this stage consists of six private companies in the sector, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No direct public comparables exist within the narrow Drones & Autonomous peer set.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to Terra Drone. The analysis identified five same-sector peers, but filtering by stage, adjacent-stage maturity, and valuation tier yielded insufficient datapoints—fewer than the three comparables required for robust median-based analysis. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial noise and misalign with Terra Drone's specific operational profile within the autonomous systems space.

Given these constraints, a reliable valuation range cannot be derived from the data provided. The analysis is limited by the absence of same-stage public benchmarks and the sparse private peer set meeting stage and scale alignment criteria. Key uncertainties include the magnitude of recent funding rounds among the five identified private peers and Terra Drone's current revenue or loss profile relative to those companies. A meaningful valuation conclusion would require either expansion of the comparable set to include adjacent subsectors or access to recent transaction multiples and funding valuations for the identified private companies.
